title: Novartis — White Space
html:
Novartis White-Space BD Map

Last updated: 2026-05-05 Purpose: Identify, prioritize, and plan pursuit of untapped revenue across Novartis brands x ZoomRx services

How to Read This Matrix

Legend:

  • ACTIVE — contracted or in-flight
  • HIGH — high priority white space (pursue within 6 months)
  • MEDIUM — medium priority (pursue within 12 months, or opportunistic)
  • LOW — low priority (long-term / speculative)
  • COMPLETED — study delivered, no active follow-up (warm lead for reactivation)
  • -- — not applicable or poor fit

The Novartis account has an unusual white-space pattern: many cells show "COMPLETED" (single HCP-Pt study done, no follow-up) rather than "ACTIVE" (recurring engagement). Each COMPLETED cell is a reactivation opportunity, not a cold start.

Oncology: Kisqali, Pluvicto, Scemblix

Service Kisqali (Breast) Pluvicto (Prostate RLT) Scemblix (CML)
PET HIGH COMPLETED (2025) COMPLETED (zero-cost, 2023)
ATU HIGH MEDIUM LOW
HCP-Patient COMPLETED (2024) COMPLETED (2024, 2025) COMPLETED (2023)
MSL / MEE MEDIUM HIGH LOW
FRM MEDIUM LOW LOW
KAM LOW LOW --
Digital Tracking MEDIUM MEDIUM LOW
OCE LOW LOW --
Message Testing HIGH MEDIUM LOW
Launch KPI -- -- --
Ferma Congress MEDIUM MEDIUM LOW
Social Listening MEDIUM MEDIUM LOW
Commercial Analytics MEDIUM MEDIUM LOW
Sales Simulation LOW LOW --
Qual LOW LOW LOW

Oncology rationale:

  • Kisqali PET is the #1 opportunity on the account. A >$4B brand in a three-way CDK4/6 race (vs. Verzenio, Ibrance) with no active promotional tracking. The completed HCP-Pt (2024) gives ZoomRx a warm lead and brand-specific proof point.
  • Pluvicto MSL/MEE is the #2 opportunity. Radioligand therapy is a novel modality requiring MSL scientific education for adoption. No other project type addresses this as directly as MSL Tracking.
  • Scemblix is lower priority. The zero-cost PET did not convert to paid in 2+ years. CML is a smaller market.

Hematology: Fabhalta, Promacta

Service Fabhalta/Iptacopan (PNH) Promacta (ITP) ITP/wAIHA/PNH (Multi)
PET HIGH LOW --
ATU HIGH LOW --
HCP-Patient COMPLETED (2024) COMPLETED (pre-2023) COMPLETED (2023)
MSL / MEE MEDIUM -- --
Digital Tracking MEDIUM LOW --
Message Testing MEDIUM -- --
Launch KPI HIGH -- --
Ferma Congress LOW -- --
Qual LOW LOW --

Hematology rationale:

  • Fabhalta PET is high priority. HCP-Pt completed (2024) — PET is the natural next step for a launch-phase oral PNH therapy competing with IV/SubQ incumbents.
  • Fabhalta Launch KPI is high priority. As a recently launched brand, Launch KPI tracking captures early adoption metrics.
  • Promacta is low priority. Mature brand with limited growth trajectory.

Cardiovascular: Entresto, Leqvio, Pelacarsen

Service Entresto (Heart Failure) Leqvio (Cholesterol) Pelacarsen (Lp(a), Pipeline)
PET HIGH HIGH MEDIUM (post-launch)
ATU HIGH HIGH MEDIUM (post-launch)
HCP-Patient HIGH COMPLETED (2023) COMPLETED (2023)
MSL / MEE MEDIUM MEDIUM MEDIUM
FRM MEDIUM LOW --
KAM LOW LOW --
Digital Tracking MEDIUM MEDIUM LOW
Message Testing HIGH MEDIUM HIGH (pre-launch)
Launch KPI -- MEDIUM HIGH (at launch)
Ferma Congress MEDIUM LOW MEDIUM
Social Listening MEDIUM MEDIUM LOW
Commercial Analytics MEDIUM LOW --
Sales Simulation LOW LOW --

Cardiovascular rationale:

  • Entresto is the single largest untapped brand on the account. At ~$7.3B [UNVERIFIED] in annual revenue, Entresto has zero ZoomRx engagement. Any entry — HCP-Pt, PET, ATU — would be transformative for account revenue. The challenge is entirely contact-related: no known relationship with the Entresto brand team.
  • Leqvio PET/ATU is high priority. HCP-Pt completed (2023) — the twice-yearly dosing model creates a unique competitive tracking opportunity vs. Repatha and Praluent.
  • Pelacarsen is medium priority (pipeline-dependent). The HCP-Pt (2023) positions ZoomRx for launch research if/when approved. Pre-launch message testing is the near-term opportunity.

Immunology: Remibrutinib/Rhapsido, Cosentyx

Service Remibrutinib/Rhapsido (CSU) Cosentyx (Psoriasis/PsA/AS)
PET ACTIVE (Wave 5) HIGH
ATU HIGH HIGH
HCP-Patient COMPLETED (2025) HIGH
MSL / MEE MEDIUM MEDIUM
FRM MEDIUM MEDIUM
Digital Tracking COMPLETED (2024) MEDIUM
Message Testing HIGH HIGH
Launch KPI -- --
Ferma Congress LOW LOW
Social Listening MEDIUM MEDIUM

Immunology rationale:

  • Rhapsido is the most-serviced brand — PET Wave 5 active, HCP-Pt and Digital Tracking completed. The next service to add is ATU or Message Testing to deepen the multi-service relationship.
  • Cosentyx is high priority across multiple services. A ~$4.5B [UNVERIFIED] brand facing biosimilar entry from 2027+ with zero ZoomRx engagement. Defensive tracking (PET, ATU) becomes urgent as patent cliff approaches. The immunology overlap with Rhapsido may mean shared contacts — leverage the Rhapsido relationship to enter Cosentyx.

Neuroscience: Kesimpta

Service Kesimpta (MS)
PET HIGH
ATU HIGH
HCP-Patient HIGH
MSL / MEE MEDIUM
Digital Tracking MEDIUM
Message Testing MEDIUM

Neuroscience rationale:

  • Kesimpta is a growing MS brand with zero ZoomRx engagement. Self-administered SubQ anti-CD20 competing with Ocrevus, Briumvi, and Tysabri. The self-administration differentiator creates distinctive patient conversations (HCP-Pt) and promotional messaging (PET) opportunities.
  • Entry requires entirely new contact development. No existing Novartis neuroscience contacts.

Top 10 Opportunities (Ranked)

Ranked by composite score of: revenue potential, ease of entry (existing relationship + proof points), and strategic value (does it unlock further deals).

Rank Brand Service Est. Revenue Entry Strategy Timeline Rationale
1 Kisqali PET $75-150K Reactivate from completed HCP-Pt (2024). Present PET proposal anchored in HCP-Pt findings about CDK4/6 competitive dynamics. 0-6 months Highest-value reactivation: >$4B brand, no active tracking, three-way CDK4/6 race demands promotional effectiveness data.
2 Pluvicto MSL/MEE Tracking $60-100K Reactivate from completed HCP-Pt + PET. Pitch MSL/MEE as the natural extension — radioligand therapy adoption is MSL-driven, not rep-driven. 0-6 months Novel modality requires MSL scientific education. No other service type addresses this. ZoomRx has MSL methodology proven at J&J.
3 Entresto HCP-Patient $75-100K Cold entry — requires new contact development on a >$7.3B brand. Use cross-TA Novartis credibility (7 years, 6 TAs) as the door-opener. 3-9 months Largest untapped brand on the account and one of the largest in all of pharma. Even a single HCP-Pt study would be transformative. Contact identification is the bottleneck.
4 Fabhalta PET $75-100K Reactivate from completed HCP-Pt (2024). PET is the natural next step for a launch-phase oral PNH brand. 0-6 months HCP-Pt done, brand is in launch phase, oral differentiation vs. IV/SubQ creates messaging that PET can track.
5 Cosentyx PET or ATU $75-100K Leverage Rhapsido/CSU contact for immunology team introduction. Pitch as biosimilar defense tracking — "what happens to Cosentyx share as biosimilars enter?" 3-9 months ~$4.5B brand facing 2027+ biosimilar cliff. Defensive tracking needs are real and urgent. May share contacts with Rhapsido team.
6 Rhapsido ATU $50-75K Upsell on existing active engagement. The PET Wave 5 relationship makes this the lowest-friction sale on the account. 0-3 months Deepens the only multi-service relationship on the account. ATU + PET = two quantitative tracking lenses on the same brand.
7 Leqvio PET $75-100K Reactivate from completed HCP-Pt (2023). Competitive CV market (vs. Repatha, Praluent) warrants promotional tracking. 3-9 months HCP-Pt done, twice-yearly dosing model creates distinctive messaging tracked well by PET.
8 Kesimpta HCP-Patient $75-100K Cold entry — requires neuroscience contact development. Self-administered MS therapy creates a compelling HCP-Pt research question. 6-12 months Growing brand with zero ZoomRx engagement. No existing neuro contacts — this is a longer-term build.
9 Entresto PET $75-150K Conditional on HCP-Pt entry (#3). If HCP-Pt is won, PET is the immediate upsell. 6-12 months If the door opens on Entresto, the revenue ceiling is very high. But depends entirely on #3 converting first.
10 Kisqali Message Testing $50-75K Conditional on PET entry (#1). Message Testing optimizes the competitive messaging tracked by PET. 6-12 months CDK4/6 competitive messaging (NATALEE vs. monarchE) is exactly what Message Testing is designed to optimize.

Revenue Summary of Top 10

Opportunity Set Low Est. High Est.
Total Top 10 $735K $1,050K
Realistic close rate (30-40%) $220K $420K
Top 5 at 50% close rate $330K $475K

Competitive Displacement Strategy

vs. Unknown Incumbent PET Vendors

Current state: ZoomRx's only active PET at Novartis is Rhapsido. Other brands (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Entresto, Cosentyx) likely use incumbent PET vendors or have no PET at all.

Displacement approach:

  1. Lead with HCP-Pt-to-PET pipeline. Every brand where ZoomRx has completed HCP-Pt is a warm PET lead. The pitch: "Your HCP-Pt study revealed how prescribers discuss your brand — a ZoomRx PET tracks whether your messaging is addressing those conversation dynamics."
  2. Use Rhapsido Wave 5 as the proof point. Five waves of longitudinal data is a powerful demonstration of PET value and ZoomRx's tracking methodology.
  3. Differentiate on AI analytics. Position PET output as AI-enhanced — faster turnaround, integrated with HCP-Pt qualitative findings, and potentially SAGAN-compatible.

vs. SAGAN (Internal Analytics)

Current state: Novartis is exploring ZoomRx data integration into SAGAN. The risk is that SAGAN replaces ZoomRx's analytical layer.

Approach:

  1. Position as the analytical intelligence layer. SAGAN is a platform; ZoomRx provides the expertise that turns data into commercial decisions.
  2. Offer structured data feeds optimized for SAGAN — but always paired with ZoomRx analytical interpretation.
  3. Demonstrate that SAGAN cannot replicate primary data collection. ZoomRx's core asset is proprietary primary research data (HCP-Pt recordings, PET physician panel) — SAGAN can analyze it but cannot generate it.

Connection to Account Strategy

The Core Challenge

Novartis is wide but shallow. The account's defining feature — 6 TAs, 12+ brands, 24 projects — masks the structural weakness: only 1 recurring tracker (Rhapsido PET), no annual contracts, and 3 of the top-5 brands untouched.

Growth Path

Phase 1 (0-6 months): Reactivate and Deepen

  • Secure Rhapsido PET Wave 6+ and add ATU (#6)
  • Reactivate Kisqali with PET proposal (#1)
  • Reactivate Pluvicto with MSL/MEE pitch (#2)
  • Reactivate Fabhalta with PET proposal (#4)
  • Clarify PSP Study Proposal status

Phase 2 (3-9 months): Enter Mega-Brands

  • Develop Entresto contact and pitch HCP-Pt (#3)
  • Leverage Rhapsido contact for Cosentyx introduction (#5)
  • Pitch Leqvio PET (#7)

Phase 3 (6-12 months): Expand Franchise

  • Enter Kesimpta via HCP-Pt (#8)
  • Deepen Entresto (PET conditional on HCP-Pt entry, #9)
  • Add Message Testing to Kisqali (#10)
  • Engage procurement with framework agreement proposal

Contact Development Required

Brand Contact Status Action
Rhapsido Active (but unnamed) Identify and document contact
Kisqali Completed HCP-Pt (warm) Reactivate contact
Pluvicto Completed HCP-Pt + PET (warm) Reactivate contact
Fabhalta Completed HCP-Pt (warm) Reactivate contact
Entresto No contact (cold) Cross-TA referral or LinkedIn research
Cosentyx No contact (cold, but immunology overlap) Leverage Rhapsido contact
Leqvio Completed HCP-Pt (warm) Reactivate contact
Kesimpta No contact (cold) New contact development required
Procurement Active evaluation (unnamed) Identify and engage proactively
SAGAN/Analytics Demo initiated (unnamed) Identify and shape integration model

Summary

The Novartis white-space map reveals one of the highest untapped revenue potentials in ZoomRx's account portfolio. Three mega-brands (Entresto ~$7.3B, Cosentyx ~$4.5B, Kesimpta) have zero engagement. Four brands with completed HCP-Pt studies (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Fabhalta, Leqvio) are warm reactivation targets for PET and other services. The only deep engagement (Rhapsido PET Wave 5) needs ATU and Message Testing added to deepen further. The top 10 opportunities total $735K-$1.05M in addressable revenue — conversion at even 30% would significantly grow this account. The bottleneck is not opportunity identification but contact identification and reactivation. The account team needs to map brand-level decision-makers, engage procurement proactively, and convert the wide-but-shallow pattern into 3-4 multi-service, recurring brand relationships.

This map should be refreshed quarterly or whenever a new brand/contact opens up. Next refresh: August 2026.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
